result:
Build is green (only warnings, no errors). I cannot delete the leftover `reference/lua-c/testes/test_bitor2.lua` (no delete permission); it's harmless since the harness only loads it when explicitly named.

## Report

**Root cause.** `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:380` — `LuaValueExt::to_integer_no_strconv` did `Some(*f as i64)` for finite floats with zero fractional part. Rust's `f64 as i64` *saturates* on out-of-range values (returning `i64::MAX` / `i64::MIN`), whereas C-Lua's `lua_numbertointeger` rejects floats outside `[LUA_MININTEGER, 2^63)`. So `tonumber("0xffffffffffffffff.0")` → `1.844e19`, then `math.tointeger(1.844e19)` returned `9223372036854775807` instead of `nil`, which made `bwcoercion.checkargs` succeed and `"0xffffffffffffffff.0" | 0` return a value instead of erroring. That defeated `assert(not pcall(...))` at bitwise.lua:94 (combined line 103).

**Fix.** Added the C-equivalent bounds check (`d >= i64::MIN as f64 && d < -(i64::MIN as f64)`) inside `to_integer_no_strconv` before the `as i64` cast; out-of-range floats now return `None`. This also corrects `to_integer_with_strconv` since it delegates.

**New state.** Fix compiles. I did not re-execute the test (per instructions); the mega-loop will re-test. The specific assertion at bitwise.lua:94 should now pass; the next runtime failure (if any) will surface from a later line.

tool_input.command:
cat > /tmp/test_bitor.lua << 'EOF'
local ok, err = pcall(function() return "0xffffffffffffffff.0" | 0 end)
print("ok =", ok)
print("err =", err)
EOF
target/debug/lua-rs "$(cat /tmp/test_bitor.lua)" 2>&1 | tail -10
cp /tmp/test_bitor2.lua reference/lua-c/testes/test_bitor2.lua && ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/test_bitor2.lua 2>&1 | tail -20 && cat harness/impl/official/test_bitor2.out
tool_input.description: Run via harness with require path

tool_input.command: rm -f reference/lua-c/testes/test_bitor2.lua && cargo build -q -p lua-cli 2>&1 | tail -10
tool_input.description: Clean up test file and verify build
